body_txt: A means of denoting an important moment within a scene by steadily and quickly reducing the image brightness until the entire screen is flat black. Its inverse, Fade In , reverses the process to bring a scene into view from a black screen. A fade from one scene to another is called a dissolve . Sometimes the screen will Fade to White instead. Extremely common in movie trailers, and sometimes used at an Act Break . Compare Fade Out , when the screen going black marks the end of a scene, not an important moment. Contrast Hat-and-Coat Shot (the smooth closing shot of a movie). Sometimes used to mean declining to describe a sex scene in textual media; for that, see Sexy Discretion Shot . However, it is very possible for them to overlap. Fade to Black was also the name of the sequel to Flashback: The Quest For Identity .
